pi_items.source: Source code analysis of python files in setup_task.sh

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a fully synthetic Python-based 3D rasterizer. The setup script dynamically generates a 3D Torus object (.obj) and instantiates the rasterizer source code (geometry.py, camera.py, rasterizer.py, render.py) injected with 5 specific mathematical bugs (barycentric coordinate miscalculation, perspective divide failure on Z, Y-axis viewport inversion, reversed winding order in normal calculation, and reversed z-buffer depth testing). A hidden test suite (test_math.py) is also generated to evaluate each of these mathematical fixes independently. The verifier checks that at least 3 out of 5 tests pass (score >= 60) and that the output.png file is successfully regenerated by render.py.
